message:
feat(search/asset_state): add maintenance event log + yield/pause signaling for multi-actor coordination (ibuuh.22)

Extend the maintenance coordination layer with a rich per-job event
log, an inter-actor yield/pause signal file, and the unified
multi-actor view those two surfaces combine to power. Builds on
ibuuh.21 (single-flight + attach-to-progress + fail-open decisions)
so that when multiple cass actors race for the same maintenance
window, they can negotiate — not just serialize — their work.

New constants:
- MAINTENANCE_EVENTS_FILE = ".maintenance-events.jsonl" — one JSON
  line per event, rotated at MAX_EVENT_LOG_ENTRIES = 500 so the log
  stays bounded even over long-running workspaces.
- YIELD_SIGNAL_FILE = "maintenance-yield.signal" — a sentinel file
  an actor can drop to request a peer to pause; peer polls and
  cooperates during natural phase boundaries.

New types (`pub(crate)`, gated behind `#[cfg_attr(not(test),
allow(dead_code))]` because only the maintenance coordinator reads
them outside tests):
- MaintenanceEvent { timestamp_ms, job_id, actor_pid, kind } —
  serde-serialized to the JSONL log.
- MaintenanceEventKind with 7 variants:
    Started { job_kind, phase }
    PhaseChanged { from, to }
    Progress { processed, total }
    YieldRequested { requester_pid, reason }
    Paused { reason }
    Resumed { reason }
    Completed { outcome }
  Variants deliberately carry the minimum payload needed to
  reconstruct "who did what when" from the log alone — no full job
  parameters (those already live in the maintenance-job.json file).

Operations added (internal):
- Append-an-event with timestamp+pid capture.
- Tail-N events for the unified multi-actor view.
- Probe YIELD_SIGNAL_FILE presence and consume atomically (remove on
  ack) so a paused peer can tell the difference between "requested"
  and "already handled".
- Rotate the JSONL log when it exceeds MAX_EVENT_LOG_ENTRIES by
  truncating the oldest half — bounded memory, bounded disk.

No external API surface changed yet; this commit lays the data
structures and I/O primitives. Wiring into the actual coordination
decision site (evaluate_maintenance_coordination /
decide_maintenance_action) is ibuuh.23.
